tickets.title: US001 (Prerequisite Check)
tickets.description:
As a: Student
I want to: get warnings when adding a course without completing its prerequisites
So that: I can avoid planning an invalid course sequence.

- [ ] Pass: System checks academic history for required prerequisites.
- [ ] Pass: Displays warning modal if prerequisite is missing.
- [ ] Pass: Marks course with warning icon but allows keeping it.

tickets.title: US002 (Drag-and-Drop)
tickets.description:
As a: Student
I want to: drag and drop courses from catalog into a visual weekly calendar
So that: I can easily see my schedule and identify time conflicts.

- [ ] Pass: Features an interactive Monday-Friday weekly calendar.
- [ ] Pass: Dropping a course places it into the correct time slot.
- [ ] Pass: Highlights overlapping courses in red as a conflict.

tickets.title: US003 (8:00 AM Alert)
tickets.description:
As a: Student

I want to: receive an alert when I add an 8:00 AM class to my schedule

So that: I can avoid oversleeping and find later sections.

- [ ] Pass: Triggers a warning banner for any 8:00 AM course added.
- [ ] Pass: Styles early morning slots with a yellow border.
- [ ] Pass: Allows student to dismiss warning and keep the course.

tickets.title: US004 (Advisor Override)
tickets.description:
As a: Academic Advisor

I want to: manually override prerequisite blocks for specific students

So that: they can enroll in required courses with special permission.

- [ ] Pass: Advisor can click an "Override" button on locked courses.
- [ ] Pass: Requires selecting a reason and typing a description.
- [ ] Pass: Logs Advisor ID, Student ID, and timestamp to database.

tickets.title: US005 (4-Year Plan)
tickets.description:
As a: Student

I want to: plan my academic roadmap up to 4 years in advance

So that: I can visualize my graduation path regardless of seat capacity.

- [ ] Pass: Interface allows adding terms up to 4 years ahead.
- [ ] Pass: Bypasses live capacity checks for future terms.
- [ ] Pass: Clearly distinguishes Active Term from Future Draft.

analysis.ambiguity1: The Head of Advising states, "The system needs to stop them from even asking [about prerequisites]." This requirement defines a desired business outcome rather than a specific system behavior or UI treatment. It is vague because it does not clarify how the system should prevent students from asking. Should the system completely hide ineligible courses from the catalog (which could cause confusion and prompt more questions), or should it display them as locked/greyed out with a clear visual tooltip explaining the missing prerequisites?
analysis.ambiguity2: The Head of Advising states, "override the system manually if a student has special permission." This requirement is vague because the term "special permission" is completely undefined. The notes do not clarify what qualifies as special permission (e.g., GPA thresholds, advisor discretion, dean's waiver), who holds the authority to grant it, or what administrative workflow and interface are required for an advisor to authenticate and apply this manual override.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: The Registrar requires that students cannot add a course to their roadmap if it is full (requiring real-time database validation), whereas the Head of Advising wants students to plan four years in advance, during which future class capacity is unpredictable and unknown.

Proposed Solution: Implement a dual-state roadmap system:

Planning Mode (Long-Term Roadmap): For future semesters, allow students to place any course on their 4-year schedule as an advisory plan, ignoring current capacity. The system uses historical/estimated schedules rather than live databases.

Registration Mode (Current Semester): For the active/upcoming registration term, link the roadmap directly to the live database. If a student attempts to enroll in a full class, the system displays a clear warning/alert prompting them to select an alternative section or join a waitlist, enforcing capacity limits without blocking future planning.
analysis.nfr1: Student data is sensitive. The system must enforce strict role-based access control (RBAC) ensuring students can only access their own data, and under no circumstances can any student view another student's schedule.
analysis.nfr2: For administrative transparency and audit compliance, the system must automatically log every instance where an advisor manually overrides a prerequisite, recording the advisor's ID, student's ID, timestamp, and target course.
analysis.nfr3: To accommodate student usability preferences, the catalog interface must feature an interactive drag-and-drop calendar for course scheduling and automatically trigger proactive visual warnings if a student selects a class scheduled for 8:00 AM.
